title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats
text: safe way store Spectra objects interoperable formats.

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats
text: MsStash defines framework flexible, language-agnostic import export formats mass spectrometry (MS) data objects R. StashSpectra package implements framework Spectra objects data backends.

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats
text: 📦 Export/import Spectra objects related MsBackend implementations across interoperable file formats 🧩 Modular design via S4 parameter classes generic methods 🔄 Fully integrated Bioconductor’s alabaster.base package.

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats
text: Tab-delimited export/import key objects:

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: MsBackendHdf5Peaks classes can stashed (read ) plain text file-based alabaster-based formats using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions combined PlainTextParam AlabasterParam parameter objects, respectively. cases, data files stored specified directory. default, backend's spectra metadata stored folder. Setting parameter consolidate = TRUE also copy HDF5-format peaks data files (containing m/z intensity values) backend folder generating self-consistent stash. Details stored files provided sections .

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks' saveObject(x, path, consolidate = FALSE, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: object MsBackendHdf5Peaks object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. consolidate logical(1) whether addition spectra metadata also peaks data file (HDF5 format) stored stash folder. Default consolidate = FALSE. spectraPath readMsObject(): optional character(1) path peaks data HDF5-format (case longer available folder referred original stashed MsBackendHdf5Peaks object). x MsBackendHdf5Peaks object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored . ... Currently ignored.

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: readMsObject() returns Spectra::MsBackendHdf5Peaks` object.

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: MsBackendHdf5Peaks stash default contain spectra metadata (spectra variables) m/z intensity values. reference original HDF5-format peaks data files stored spectra variable dataStorage. files moved stash copied different computer, path original data files needs provided readMsObject() function parameter spectraPath. Alternatively, consolidate = TRUE, also possible copy peaks data files stash directory generating self-contained data stash. Note however case two copies data files exist (original location stash directory).

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: saveMsObject() function PlainTextParam stores spectra metadata (spectra variables) MsBackendHdf5Peaks plain tabulator delimited text file name ms_backend_spectra_data.txt directory specified parameter path PlainTextParam object. Depending parameter consolidate also peaks data files (HDF5 format) copied stash folder (consolidate = TRUE).

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: AlabasterParam, spectra metadata exported (imported) alabaster framework. Similar PlainTextParam, consolidate = TRUE also copy HDF5-format peaks data files stash directory.

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
text: Johannes Rainer

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5Peaks-stash
code: library(Spectra) #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #> Loading required package: generics #>  #> Attaching package: ‘generics’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff, #>     setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: ‘BiocGenerics’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following object is masked from ‘package:utils’: #>  #>     data #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     Filter, Find, Map, Position, Reduce, anyDuplicated, aperm, append, #>     as.data.frame, basename, cbind, colnames, dirname, do.call, #>     duplicated, eval, evalq, get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, #>     mapply, match, mget, order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, #>     rank, rbind, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, scale, sequence, table, #>     tapply, transform, unique, unsplit, which.max, which.min #>  #> Attaching package: ‘S4Vectors’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:utils’: #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     I, expand.grid, unname #> Loading required package: BiocParallel library(StashSpectra) ## Create an example MsBackendHdf5Peaks backend from a single mzML file library(MsDataHub) tmp <- backendInitialize(MsBackendMzR(), X20171016_POOL_POS_1_105.134.mzML()) #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache be_h5 <- backendInitialize(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(), data = spectraData(tmp),     hdf5path = file.path(tempdir(), "h5_backend")) be_h5 #> MsBackendHdf5Peaks with 931 spectra #>       msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>     <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1           1     0.280         1 #> 2           1     0.559         2 #> 3           1     0.838         3 #> 4           1     1.117         4 #> 5           1     1.396         5 #> ...       ...       ...       ... #> 927         1   258.641       927 #> 928         1   258.920       928 #> 929         1   259.199       929 #> 930         1   259.478       930 #> 931         1   259.757       931 #>  ... 34 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #>  54a78d9963c_7859.h5  d <- file.path(tempdir(), "example_hdf5") ptp <- PlainTextParam(path = d)  ## Store the object into the stash, including the peaks data files. saveMsObject(be_h5, ptp, consolidate = TRUE)  ## List the content of the folder: ms_backend_spectra_data.txt file ## with the spectra metadata and an HDF5 file with the peaks data: dir(d) #> [1] "54a78d9963c_7859.h5"         "ms_backend_spectra_data.txt"  ## Restore the stashed object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(), ptp)  ## Store the object in an alabaster-format stash d <- file.path(tempdir(), "example_hdf5_2")  ap <- AlabasterParam(d) saveMsObject(be_h5, ap)  ## Check the content of the stash; with the default (`consolidate = FALSE`) ## no HDF5 data file was moved. dir(d) #> [1] "OBJECT"            "_environment.json" "mod_count"         #> [4] "spectra_data"       ## Restore the object again res <- readMsObject(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(), ap) res #> MsBackendHdf5Peaks with 931 spectra #>       msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>     <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1           1     0.280         1 #> 2           1     0.559         2 #> 3           1     0.838         3 #> 4           1     1.117         4 #> 5           1     1.396         5 #> ...       ...       ...       ... #> 927         1   258.641       927 #> 928         1   258.920       928 #> 929         1   259.199       929 #> 930         1   259.478       930 #> 931         1   259.757       931 #>  ... 25 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #>  54a78d9963c_7859.h5

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: MsBackendMzR classes can stashed (read ) plain text file-based alabaster-based formats using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions combined PlainTextParam AlabasterParam parameter objects, respectively. Setting parameter consolidate = TRUE saveMsObject() saveObject() function copy also original MS data files folder generating self-consistent stash. Additional properties stash formats described detail sections .

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR' saveObject(x, path, consolidate = FALSE, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: object MsBackendMzR object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. consolidate logical(1) whether addition spectra metadata also original MS data files stored stash folder. Default consolidate = FALSE. spectraPath readMsObject(): optional character(1) path MS data files (mzML, mzXML CDF) case longer available folder referred original stashed MsBackendMzR object. x MsBackendMzR object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored . ... Currently ignored.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: readMsObject() returns Spectra::MsBackendMzR` object.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: MsBackendMzR objects contain peaks data (.e., m/z intensity values) retrieve original MS data files (mzML, mzXML CDF format). Unless consolidate = TRUE used, MsBackendMzR stash therefore contain spectra metadata (.e., spectra variables) peaks data. reference original MS data files stored spectra variable dataStorage files longer available directory specified dataStorage restored object valid, unless new location provided parameter spectraPath.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: saveMsObject() function PlainTextParam stores spectra metadata (spectra variables) MsBackendMzR plain tabulator delimited text file name ms_backend_spectra_data.txt directory specified parameter path PlainTextParam object. Importantly, peaks data (m/z intensity values) exported saveMsObject(). readMsObject() restores previously stashed MsBackendMzR object directory specified parameter path PlainTextParam. additional parameter spectraPath readMsObject() allows define path MS data files containing full MS data (.e., mzML, mzXML CDF files referred MsBackendMzR).

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: saveMsObject() AlabasterParam parameter object stashes provided MsBackendMzR object alabster-based format directory defined argument param AlabasterParam. readMsObject() AlabasterParam restores previously stashed MsBackend object. Optional parameter spectraPath allows specify storage path MS data files referenced MsBackendMzR (case longer directory saving object). addition, alabaster methods saveObject() readObject() can used save read MsBackendMzR objects.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
text: Philippine Louail, Johannes Rainer

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzR-stash
code: library(StashSpectra) library(Spectra) library(MsDataHub)  ## Create a MsBackendMzR from test data be <- backendInitialize(MsBackendMzR(), PestMix1_DDA.mzML()) #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache be #> MsBackendMzR with 7602 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.231         1 #> 2            1     0.351         2 #> 3            1     0.471         3 #> 4            1     0.591         4 #> 5            1     0.711         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 7598         1   899.491      7598 #> 7599         1   899.613      7599 #> 7600         1   899.747      7600 #> 7601         1   899.872      7601 #> 7602         1   899.993      7602 #>  ... 34 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 54a775c0fa5_7861  ## Define a folder where to stash the object pth <- file.path(tempdir(), "mzr_stash")  ## Stash the object to this folder in a plain text-based format saveMsObject(be, PlainTextParam(pth))  ## Restore the stashed object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendMzR(), PlainTextParam(pth)) res #> MsBackendMzR with 7602 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.231         1 #> 2            1     0.351         2 #> 3            1     0.471         3 #> 4            1     0.591         4 #> 5            1     0.711         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 7598         1   899.491      7598 #> 7599         1   899.613      7599 #> 7600         1   899.747      7600 #> 7601         1   899.872      7601 #> 7602         1   899.993      7602 #>  ... 27 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 54a775c0fa5_7861  ## Clean-up unlink(pth, recursive = TRUE)  ## Store the data in *alabaster* format including also the original MS ## data files (`consolidate = TRUE`) saveMsObject(be, AlabasterParam(pth), consolidate = TRUE)  ## Get the directory content of the stash folder: dir(pth) #> [1] "54a775c0fa5_7861"  "OBJECT"            "_environment.json" #> [4] "spectra_data"       ## Restore the object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendMzR(), AlabasterParam(pth)) res #> MsBackendMzR with 7602 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.231         1 #> 2            1     0.351         2 #> 3            1     0.471         3 #> 4            1     0.591         4 #> 5            1     0.711         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 7598         1   899.491      7598 #> 7599         1   899.613      7599 #> 7600         1   899.747      7600 #> 7601         1   899.872      7601 #> 7602         1   899.993      7602 #>  ... 27 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 54a775c0fa5_7861  ## If the data is exported with `consolidate = FALSE` (the default), the ## new location of MS data files could be provided with parameter ## `spectraPath` of the `readMsObject()` function in case they are no ## longer in the path referenced by the stashed object.  ## Clean-up unlink(pth, recursive = TRUE)
